liveStatus.summary: /api/health degraded LIVE isLive=true runtimeBankroll=3.735043 pendingBuys=0 pendingSettlements=0; /api/status risk.tradingPaused=true; /api/wallet/balance LIVE tradingBalance=3.735043 source=CONFIRMED_CONSERVATIVE_MIN tradeReady=true activeTimeframes=15m; /api/diagnostics available=true; checkedAt=2026-04-28T04:19:08Z

correction.latencyMethod: For latencyShiftMinutes > 0, the event minute/timestamp, signalEntryPrice, orderPrice, oppositePrice, edge gate, hard cap, and pre-resolution exit are recomputed from the delayed minute snapshot.

approaches.name: SOL H20 Seed Expansion
approaches.logic: Expand the prior SOL H20 sparse lead around UTC hour 20 using train-only selection and fresh chronological holdout.

approaches.name: Time-of-Day Volatility Regime
approaches.logic: Cluster-like proxy: only mine hours where the selected side shows large early realized range before entry.

approaches.name: Theta Decay Final-Minute Sniping
approaches.logic: Buy the side already in a profitable mid-price band during minutes 10-13 and require historical pre-resolution exit evidence.

approaches.name: Low-Entry Convexity Harvest
approaches.logic: Search lower entry bands where a win has high unit ROI and require Wilson-LCB EV after fees and slippage.

approaches.name: Adversarial Spread-Convergence Proxy
approaches.logic: Use yes/no minute prices as a historical orderbook proxy and accept only tight yes+no convergence.

approaches.name: High-LCB Sparse Spread-Convergence
approaches.logic: Restrict spread-convergence to sparse train-only high Wilson-LCB rules before any holdout evaluation.

approaches.name: L2 Print-Imbalance Proxy
approaches.logic: Treat trade-print imbalance as a proxy for executable pressure and buy only when target prints dominate opposite prints.

approaches.name: Early Micro-Breakout Follow
approaches.logic: Trade minutes 1-4 only when the side price moved up from minute 0 and continues upward into an affordable band.

approaches.name: Adversarial Inversion of Expensive Consensus
approaches.logic: When one side is expensive late in-cycle, buy the cheap opposite side only if it remains convex and affordable.

approaches.name: Previous-Cycle Streak Fade
approaches.logic: If the same asset resolved the same way in the previous two cycles, buy the opposite direction in a tradable band.

approaches.name: Cross-Asset Previous-Majority Latency Proxy
approaches.logic: Use the previous 15m cycle majority direction across BTC/ETH/SOL/XRP as a latency proxy for the next cycle direction.

approaches.name: One-Minute Momentum Reprice
approaches.logic: Enter only when the latest minute price has risen versus the prior minute, then stress with actual delayed snapshot repricing.

approaches.name: Open-Reversal Convexity
approaches.logic: Buy a side that sold off sharply from minute 0 into a low-price convex band, testing whether early overreaction reverses.

approaches.name: Multi-Minute In-Cycle Momentum Path
approaches.logic: Require a two-minute monotonic side-price climb and mine the path feature rather than a single static minute.

approaches.name: Pre-Resolution Exit Harvest
approaches.logic: Mine entries whose historical winners often reached a 95c pre-resolution exit window before settlement.

approaches.name: Cross-Asset Leader Follow
approaches.logic: Use BTC/ETH/SOL/XRP resolution from one or two prior cycles as a leader signal for the current asset.

approaches.name: Cross-Asset Leader Fade
approaches.logic: Invert prior-cycle leader signals to test whether apparent cross-asset continuation is a trap.

approaches.name: Three-Cycle Streak Follow
approaches.logic: After three same-direction same-asset resolutions, test continuation in tradable mid-price bands.

approaches.name: Three-Cycle Streak Fade
approaches.logic: After three same-direction same-asset resolutions, buy the opposite side when convex and affordable.

approaches.name: 4h Bias Filters 15m Follow
approaches.logic: Use only the previous completed 4h resolution as a directional filter for current 15m entries.

approaches.name: 4h Bias Filters 15m Fade
approaches.logic: Fade the previous completed 4h resolution when the opposite 15m side is still convex and tradable.

approaches.name: Adversarial Opposite-Breakout Fade
approaches.logic: Find early opposite-side breakouts that historically fail, then buy the cheap fading side.

approaches.name: 5m Tight Spread-Convergence
approaches.logic: Mine 5m cycles for early tight yes/no convergence in executable mid-price bands.

approaches.name: 5m Micro Momentum Reprice
approaches.logic: Enter only when a 5m side has strengthened versus the previous minute, then stress with delayed-minute repricing.

approaches.name: 5m Print-Imbalance Proxy
approaches.logic: Use 5m trade-print imbalance as a lightweight order-flow proxy in affordable bands.

approaches.name: 5m Previous-Cycle Streak Fade
approaches.logic: Fade two same-direction resolved 5m cycles on the same asset when the opposite side is still tradable.

approaches.name: 4h Intracycle Momentum
approaches.logic: Trade 4h mid-cycle momentum only when the side has improved versus the previous minute and remains below the hard cap.

approaches.name: 4h Spread-Convergence Proxy
approaches.logic: Use refreshed 4h yes/no minute convergence as a historical proxy for executable consensus.

approaches.name: 4h Low-Entry Convexity
approaches.logic: Search 4h lower entry bands where a win can materially accelerate micro-bankroll compounding.

verdict: NO-GO: best deployable approach spread_convergence_orderbook_proxy failed the corrected micro-bankroll gate under repriced latency ($5 bust 28.86%, $7 bust 13.60%, $10 strict median $13.55, adverse bust 11.94%).
